text: Prices for most goods and services should be set by markets rather than by government.
The government should own and operate key industries such as energy and rail.
Government regulation of business generally does more harm than good.
Antitrust enforcement against large corporations should be expanded.
There should be no legally mandated minimum wage.
Utilities such as water and electricity should be publicly owned.
Private companies deliver most services more efficiently than government agencies.
The state should subsidize strategically important industries.
Rent prices should be capped by law in high-cost cities.
Occupational licensing requirements should be reduced.
A maximum legal limit should be placed on personal wealth.
Corporate taxes should be lowered to encourage investment.
Banks should face stricter government oversight.
Land is best allocated when it can be bought and sold like any other commodity.
The government should guarantee a universal basic income.
Welfare benefits for those able to work should be conditional on work or job-seeking.
Healthcare should be provided through a single government program covering everyone.
Public assistance programs make long-term dependence more likely.
Tuition at public universities should be free.
Individuals, not government, hold primary responsibility for their own economic security.
Eligibility requirements for public assistance should be stricter than they are now.
Government should provide free childcare for working parents.
Social Security benefits should be expanded.
Charities and community organizations handle poverty relief better than government programs.
No one should go without basic necessities, regardless of whether they work.
Unemployment benefits should be time-limited.
The federal government should guarantee housing for all citizens.
Applicants for public assistance should be required to pass drug tests.
Tariffs on imported goods are an appropriate way to sustain domestic industries.
Free trade agreements benefit the country overall.
Goods vital to national security should be manufactured domestically even at higher cost.
Consumers benefit when companies can buy from the cheapest supplier worldwide.
Companies that move production overseas should face financial penalties.
Trade restrictions raise prices more than they help workers.
Domestic-content requirements should apply to all government purchases.
International supply chains make the economy more resilient, not less.
A country should aim to produce most of what it consumes.
Trade policy should favor exporters even if imported goods become more expensive.
Membership in international trade organizations serves the national interest.
Foreign competition improves the quality of domestic products.
Critical medicines and minerals should be sourced domestically regardless of cost.
Tariffs are taxes paid mainly by domestic consumers.
Society benefits when marriage is defined as between one man and one woman.
A person's gender identity can differ from their biological sex.
Traditional gender roles reflect real differences between men and women.
Children develop best when raised by a mother and a father.
Recreational drug use by adults is a private matter that should not be a crime.
Popular culture has changed for the worse over the past several decades.
Prostitution between consenting adults should be legal.
Same-sex couples should be able to adopt children on the same terms as other couples.
Abortion should be prohibited in most or all circumstances.
Pornography should be more difficult to access than it is now.
Casual attitudes toward sex have harmed society.
People should be able to use whichever pronouns they choose without social penalty.
Divorce should be harder to obtain than it currently is.
Physician-assisted death should be legal for terminally ill adults.
Religious values should inform public policy.
Government and religion should remain strictly separate.
Public schools should be permitted to include prayer in school activities.
Religious displays should be allowed on government property.
A shared religious foundation makes a society stronger.
Religious organizations should be eligible to deliver public services with government funding.
Morality does not depend on religious belief.
Clergy and religious institutions should stay out of political debates.
Religious exemptions from generally applicable laws should be broad.
A public official's religious beliefs are irrelevant to their fitness for office.
Declining religious practice has weakened the country's moral fabric.
Tax exemptions for religious organizations should be ended.
Faith-based schools should receive public funding on equal terms with secular schools.
Laws should be justified without reference to any religion.
When public order and individual freedom conflict, public order should prevail.
The government should not be able to tell adults what risks they may take with their own bodies.
Emergencies justify temporary suspension of ordinary civil liberties.
Seatbelt and helmet laws are appropriate uses of government power.
People should be free to make choices others consider immoral, so long as no one else is harmed.
Curfews are an acceptable tool for maintaining public safety.
Private gun ownership should face few restrictions.
The state may require vaccination during a public health emergency.
What consenting adults do in private is not the government's business.
Strong leadership sometimes matters more than strict adherence to procedure.
Government should have the power to ban organizations it deems dangerous.
Civil disobedience is sometimes justified.
A national identification card should be required for all citizens.
Certain individual rights should not be subject to majority vote.
Government monitoring of communications is acceptable if it prevents serious crime.
Privacy from government observation is a right that should rarely be overridden.
Law enforcement should be able to access encrypted messages with a warrant.
Widespread camera surveillance of public spaces makes cities safer.
Government surveillance poses little risk to law-abiding people.
Companies should be barred from collecting personal data without explicit consent.
Police should not use facial recognition technology.
Airport-style security screening is a reasonable price for safety.
Government should never maintain lists of citizens based on their beliefs.
Intelligence agencies need broad latitude to operate in secret.
Anonymous use of the internet should remain possible.
Financial transactions should be traceable by authorities to combat crime.
Employees who leak classified government programs should be prosecuted.
Strong encryption available to everyone benefits society more than it costs.
Speech that demeans religious or ethnic groups should be against the law.
Offensive and even hateful speech should remain legal.
Social media platforms should be required to remove false information.
The government should never determine what counts as misinformation.
Burning the national flag should be a protected form of expression.
Speech that glorifies violence should be restricted, not only speech that directly incites it.
Universities should be able to bar speakers whose views offend parts of the student body.
The remedy for harmful speech is more speech, not restriction.
Advertising designed to manipulate emotions should be regulated.
Obscenity laws should be enforced more strictly.
Anonymous political speech deserves the same protection as attributed speech.
Defamation should be easier to prove in court than it currently is.
Blasphemy should carry no legal consequence.
Political dissent must remain legal even when it undermines public confidence in government.
The primary purpose of prison is punishment.
Most prisoners can be rehabilitated and returned to society.
The death penalty is an appropriate punishment for the worst crimes.
Mandatory minimum sentences should be abolished.
Harsh sentences deter crime.
Drug addiction should be treated as a health issue rather than a criminal one.
People who complete their sentences should regain full civil rights, including voting.
Police should have greater latitude to stop and search people in high-crime areas.
Juveniles who commit serious crimes should be tried as adults.
Prison conditions in this country are too harsh.
Life sentences for repeat offenders under three-strikes laws are just.
Long-term solitary confinement should be banned.
Restorative justice programs should replace incarceration for some offenses.
It is better that some guilty people go free than that innocent people are imprisoned.
Voting should require demonstrating basic knowledge of how government works.
Every adult citizen should have an equal vote regardless of education or knowledge.
Important national questions should be decided by public referendum.
Judges should be appointed rather than elected.
Complex policy decisions are better made by qualified experts than by popular vote.
The voting age should be lowered.
Constitutional limits should override the will of the majority.
People convicted of serious crimes should permanently lose the right to vote.
Voting should be mandatory.
Longer terms of office would let leaders govern with less regard for short-term opinion.
Randomly selected citizens' assemblies should help decide major public issues.
National leaders should be chosen by direct popular vote without intermediary institutions.
Some public offices should require professional qualifications, as many private jobs do.
The country would be governed better if fewer, better-informed people voted.
Elections in this country are generally administered fairly.
Public officials mostly serve themselves rather than the public.
The court system usually reaches just outcomes.
Mainstream news organizations report accurately more often than not.
Official government statistics can generally be believed.
A small group of powerful people effectively controls the government regardless of elections.
The political system is capable of reforming itself through normal processes.
Scientific institutions can be trusted to follow evidence rather than politics.
Most civil servants are competent professionals.
The two-party system serves the country adequately.
Regulatory agencies mostly serve the industries they are supposed to oversee.
Official accounts of major events frequently conceal the truth.
Political donations effectively buy policy outcomes.
Society's problems require sweeping change rather than gradual reform.
Gradual reform through existing institutions is the most reliable path to improvement.
Sometimes a system must be dismantled before something better can be built.
Revolutions usually produce worse outcomes than the systems they replace.
Compromise with political opponents is usually a mistake.
Stability is worth preserving even at the cost of slower progress.
If constitutional processes block necessary change, extraordinary measures can be justified.
Political violence is never acceptable in this country.
The founding structures of American government need fundamental redesign.
It is wiser to repair institutions than to replace them.
Urgent problems justify bypassing slow deliberative processes.
Radical ideas deserve serious consideration even when they unsettle people.
Working within the system produces more lasting results than working against it.
National problems require national solutions set at the federal level.
Decisions should be made at the most local level capable of handling them.
States should set their own policies on most contested social issues.
Federal standards should override conflicting state laws.
Education policy is best set by local school boards rather than the federal government.
Fifty different sets of state rules is an inefficient way to run a modern country.
The federal government has taken on responsibilities that belong to the states.
Civil rights protections must be uniform nationwide rather than left to the states.
States should be able to opt out of federal programs.
Local police should operate independently of federal oversight.
National licensing standards should replace state-by-state professional licensing.
The constitutional division of power currently tilts too far toward the states.
Cities and counties should have more autonomy from their state governments.
Interstate infrastructure justifies a strong federal role in planning and funding.
When national interests and international cooperation conflict, national interests should come first.
Being a citizen of the world matters as much as being a citizen of one's country.
National sovereignty should never be ceded to international organizations.
International law should bind all countries, including this one.
Pride in one's country is a virtue.
Patriotism too easily becomes an excuse for ignoring a country's faults.
The United Nations does more good than harm.
A shared national identity matters more than the sum of separate group identities.
Foreign aid spending should be cut substantially.
Humanity's long-term future lies in cooperation that transcends nations.
The national anthem and flag deserve formal respect in public life.
Multinational institutions should be able to sanction countries that violate agreed rules.
History education should emphasize what unites the nation rather than its failings.
A world government would be a desirable long-term goal.
Overall immigration levels should be reduced.
Immigration strengthens the country more than it strains it.
People residing in the country unlawfully should be deported.
Long-term residents who entered unlawfully should have a path to citizenship.
Immigrants should be required to learn English.
Refugee admissions should be expanded.
Skilled-worker immigration benefits the economy.
Immigration policy should favor applicants from culturally similar countries.
Children born here to parents residing unlawfully should not receive automatic citizenship.
Diversity of national origins makes communities stronger.
Employers who hire unauthorized workers should face severe penalties.
Border enforcement matters more than expanding legal immigration pathways.
Immigration detention should be used only as a last resort.
Concern about rapid demographic change is legitimate.
The military should be used to advance the country's interests abroad, not only to defend its territory.
The country should avoid foreign wars unless directly attacked.
Maintaining the world's strongest military is essential.
Overseas military bases should be substantially reduced.
Promoting democracy abroad is a legitimate goal of foreign policy.
Other countries' internal affairs are not this country's business.
Military alliances such as NATO serve the national interest.
Military spending should be cut.
Economic sanctions are an appropriate tool against hostile governments.
Wars of choice in recent decades have made the country less safe.
When this country withdraws from a region, worse actors tend to fill the vacuum.
Defense commitments to allies should be honored even at high cost.
Diplomacy should nearly always be exhausted before force is considered.
The country should be willing to act militarily without allies when necessary.
New technologies should be adopted quickly; problems can be corrected as they arise.
New technologies should be restricted until they are proven safe.
Artificial intelligence will do more good than harm.
Genetically modified crops are safe and beneficial.
Genetic engineering of human embryos should remain prohibited even if it could prevent disease.
The benefits of automation outweigh the jobs it displaces.
Social media has damaged society more than it has helped.
Nuclear power generation should be expanded.
Space exploration deserves major public and private investment.
Some technologies are too dangerous to develop at all.
Technological progress is the main driver of human improvement.
Children's access to smartphones and social platforms should be sharply limited.
Self-driving vehicles should be permitted on public roads now.
Regulators should slow innovation in fast-moving fields until laws catch up.
Economic growth should take priority over environmental restrictions.
Protecting the environment is worth accepting slower economic growth.
Climate change is among the most serious problems facing the country.
Environmental regulations cost more jobs than they are worth.
Fossil fuel production should expand to keep energy affordable.
A tax on carbon emissions should be implemented.
Predictions of environmental catastrophe have repeatedly proven exaggerated.
Endangered-species protections should yield when they block needed development.
Meat consumption should be reduced for environmental reasons.
Public lands should be opened to more mining, drilling, and logging.
The country should aim for net-zero carbon emissions by mid-century.
Environmental quality has improved over recent decades, and markets deserve much of the credit.
The environmental interests of future generations should constrain today's consumption.
International climate agreements burden this country unfairly.
Government should set prices for essential goods and services.
Major employers should be required to include worker representatives on their boards.
Inheritance above a certain amount should be taxed heavily.
The economy works best when government's role is limited to enforcing contracts and property rights.
Assistance to the poor is a duty of government, not merely an option.
Able-bodied adults should not receive long-term government support.
A family's access to healthcare should not depend on its income.
Private savings, not public programs, should fund retirement.
Consumers should be able to buy foreign goods without added import taxes.
Protecting domestic manufacturing justifies higher consumer prices.
Trade with rival nations should be restricted even when it is profitable.
Reducing trade barriers has benefited this country over the past half century.
Society was right to expand the definition of marriage.
Communities benefit when most people follow long-established customs.
Raising children within marriage should be encouraged over other arrangements.
Cultural change over recent decades has been mostly for the better.
Public life would be better with less religious influence.
Public officials should be able to cite their faith when explaining their votes.
Religious instruction should be available within public school hours for families that want it.
National mottos and oaths should not reference God.
Police should need a warrant to track a person's location over time.
Collecting bulk data on citizens is acceptable if it is rarely examined.
Security agencies should disclose their surveillance programs to the public.
Cash should remain available as a form of untraceable payment.
People should face legal consequences for publicly denying well-documented historical atrocities.
Even speech most people find repugnant should be answered with argument, not law.
Platforms, not government, should decide what content they host.
Threatening or harassing individuals online should be prosecuted more aggressively.
The primary purpose of prison is rehabilitation.
Sentences in this country are generally too lenient.
Nonviolent offenders should rarely be incarcerated.
Public safety justifies detaining defendants before trial when they appear dangerous.
A single national policy on abortion is preferable to state-by-state variation.
State governments understand local needs better than the federal government does.
Federal minimum standards should apply to elections in every state.
States should be free to experiment with policies the federal government opposes.
International cooperation should take precedence over national advantage when they conflict.
This country owes more to its own citizens than to people elsewhere.
National symbols deserve legal protection from desecration.
Decisions of international courts should carry weight in this country's own courts.
It is healthy for citizens to feel their country is special.
Current immigration levels should be maintained or increased.
Legal immigration should be selected primarily by skills and qualifications.
Communities should have a say in how many newcomers settle there.
Children brought here unlawfully by their parents should be able to stay.
Military force is sometimes warranted even when the country has not been attacked.
This country should reduce security guarantees to wealthy allies who can defend themselves.
Standing aside during foreign atrocities is sometimes the right choice.
A forward military presence abroad prevents wars rather than provoking them.
On balance, the internet era has improved daily life.
Companies should be liable for harms caused by algorithms they deploy.
Human enhancement technologies should be available to those who want them.
Progress that cannot be reversed should not be rushed.
Environmental rules should be relaxed when energy prices rise sharply.
This generation is obligated to leave the natural world in better condition than it found it.
Wilderness should be preserved even where development would be profitable.
Affordable energy today matters more than emissions targets decades away.
Government may restrict personal behavior that harms only the person doing it.
Adults should be free to buy unhealthy products without taxes designed to discourage them.
Gambling should be legal for adults.
The state should promote healthy lifestyles through mandates rather than only through information.
Adults should have the final say over refusing medical treatment, even against doctors' advice.
Age limits on alcohol should be strictly enforced.
Government warnings and taxes on tobacco have been appropriate.
What adults eat, drink, and smoke is not the government's concern.
Motorcyclists should be free to ride without helmets at their own risk.
The state should be able to require treatment for people with severe addictions.
Laws should discourage vices even among consenting adults.
Personal risk-taking such as extreme sports needs no government oversight.
The executive should be able to act quickly without waiting for legislative approval in urgent matters.
Courts should be able to strike down actions of elected leaders.
Emergency powers should always carry automatic expiration dates.
A government that cannot act decisively is worse than one that occasionally overreaches.
Property may be taken for public use when fair compensation is paid.
There should be strict constitutional limits on what any government may do.
Wartime justifies government control of industry and labor.
The military should never be deployed against citizens on home soil.
Secret government programs are sometimes necessary.
Citizens should be able to sue the government on equal terms with suing anyone else.
Concentrated government power is dangerous no matter who holds it.
Citizenship alone should be sufficient qualification to vote.
Voters should be required to show government-issued identification.
People who receive most of their income from government programs should still be able to vote on those programs.
Long-term residents who are not citizens should be able to vote in local elections.
The right to vote should expand over time, not contract.
Passing the citizenship test given to immigrants would be a reasonable requirement for voting.
Sixteen-year-olds who work and pay taxes should be able to vote.
Property or taxpaying requirements for voting belong in the past.
Voting is a privilege to be earned rather than a right held automatically.
People with severe cognitive impairment should not vote.
Efforts to make voting easier, such as automatic registration, should continue.
Election outcomes would be better with fewer, more committed voters than with maximum turnout.
Central banks should set monetary policy independent of elected officials.
Major infrastructure decisions should be settled by engineers and planners rather than by ballot.
Public opinion should guide policy even when experts disagree with it.
Unelected officials hold too much power over daily life.
Regulatory details are best left to specialists rather than legislators.
Juries of ordinary citizens are better judges of guilt than panels of professionals.
National ballot initiatives should be available, as they are in many states.
Credentials matter more than popularity in choosing who runs public agencies.
When scientific consensus and majority opinion conflict, policy should follow the science.
Ordinary citizens understand their own interests better than experts do.
Most elected officials genuinely try to do what is right.
Journalists mostly try to get the facts right.
Campaign promises are made to be broken.
The news media report what powerful interests want reported.
Politicians who change positions usually do so from conviction rather than convenience.
Congress is more capable than it is given credit for.
The major political parties offer meaningfully different choices.
Lobbying is a legitimate part of representative government.
The political class lives by different rules than everyone else.
Media coverage of politics is driven more by audience than by truth.
Public criticism keeps politicians reasonably honest.
Term limits are needed because incumbents cannot be trusted to leave.
On the whole, people who run for office are no worse than the general population.
Election results in this country cannot be trusted.
The census and official statistics are maintained honestly.
Public health agencies give sound guidance most of the time.
Courts apply the law equally to rich and poor.
The tax system is enforced more strictly against ordinary people than against the wealthy.
Bureaucracies serve procedures rather than people.
Official inquiries into government failures usually reach the truth.
Institutions that investigate themselves rarely find fault.
Government databases and records are frequently wrong.
Inspectors general and auditors keep agencies honest.
The basic structure of American society is sound.
Deep problems require redesigning institutions, not just better management.
Most calls for transformation underestimate what would be lost.
The country needs changes on the scale of the New Deal or the founding era.
Tinkering at the edges cannot fix what is wrong.
Preserving what works matters more than pursuing what might work better.
Every generation should reexamine its institutions from the ground up.
The saying, if it is not broken do not fix it, applies well to government.
Ambitious reform agendas usually cause more disruption than improvement.
The gap between how things are and how they should be is vast.
Constitutional amendments should be easier to pass than they are.
Settled arrangements deserve a strong presumption in their favor.
Ends, however worthy, do not justify unlawful means.
Breaking an unjust law openly and accepting the penalty is honorable.
Institutions should be changed only through the procedures they themselves provide.
Mass strikes that halt the economy are a legitimate political tool.
Blocking roads or occupying buildings is acceptable protest.
Court orders must be obeyed even by those who believe them wrong.
History's great advances required breaking the rules of their time.
The peaceful transfer of power matters more than any policy outcome.
Sabotage of property, without harm to people, can be justified in extreme causes.
Norms that are not written into law still deserve obedience.
To show you are reading carefully, select Completely disagree for this statement.
For this statement, please select Completely agree.
This item checks attention; please select Completely agree.
